description: John Zenon, an environmental engineer, returns from a 2-week field survey with an expanding 'bullseye' rash (erythema migrans), fatigue, and a low-grade fever following a tick exposure 10 days ago. As the occupational health technician, document this workplace biological exposure and initiate the Lyme disease protocol in GNU Health. Document the Lyme disease diagnosis (ICD-10 code A69.2) and a clinical evaluation showing a low-grade fever (temperature >= 37.5 C). Prescribe an appropriate antibiotic treatment (Doxycycline, Amoxicillin, or Cefuroxime), order at least one relevant laboratory test (e.g., Lyme Serology or CBC), and schedule a follow-up appointment for rash reassessment 14 to 28 days from today. Login credentials: username 'admin', password 'gnusolidario'.
